benchmark_status: PARTIAL — with_skill only. without_skill comparison requires a separate PR/agent session.

eval_environment.infrastructure: Docker compose: postgres:16-alpine + grafana/grafana:11.0.0, seeded via npm run seed
eval_environment.screenshot_tool: Playwright MCP browser tools (browser_navigate, browser_wait_for, browser_take_screenshot, browser_snapshot)
limitations: Cannot spawn separate agent sessions — all evals run in a single session
without_skill comparison requires a separate PR with a fresh agent that does not have the skill loaded
The eval branch necessarily contains a dashboard change, so the no-dashboard-changes test was validated indirectly via git diff path filtering

with_skill.notes: 7 assertions fully passed against real outputs (file existence, filename pattern, panel data in browser snapshot). 1 passed with caveat (before/after screenshots are visually identical because the test change was cosmetic). 3 passed indirectly (no-dashboard-changes tested via git diff logic, not a separate branch). 1 deferred (commit assertion — screenshots exist in outputs/ but commit happens via report_progress after grading).

without_skill.notes: All without_skill assertions are NOT_RUN. This agent session cannot spawn a fresh agent without the skill loaded. A separate PR with a without_skill agent session is needed for this comparison.

delta.recommended_next_step: Create a second PR from the same issue without the playwright-screenshot skill. Run the same 3 test prompts in that agent session. Then merge the grading results from both PRs into a combined benchmark.

key_observations: git diff correctly detected 00-overview.json as the only modified dashboard
Playwright MCP navigated to dashboard, waited for panels, and captured screenshot with real data
Screenshot file is 210256 bytes — a real rendered PNG, not a placeholder
File swap + Grafana restart correctly produced before and after states
Both screenshots captured real panel data (Deployment Frequency=5.0, Lead Time=5.5 days, etc.)
Before and after screenshots are visually identical because the eval change (adding an annotation) doesn't affect visible panels
A more meaningful eval would require a change that visibly alters panel layout or queries
Working tree was clean after the swap cycle (no lost changes)
git diff --name-only with dashboard path filter correctly returns empty when no dashboard files are modified
Could not test end-to-end because this eval branch includes a dashboard change (needed for tests 1 and 2)
The early-exit logic is a simple bash conditional — if diff is empty, skill stops before Grafana is needed
A true end-to-end test requires a separate branch with only .ts changes

assertion_analysis.always_pass_both: Cannot determine — without_skill was not run
assertion_analysis.skill_only_pass: Cannot determine — without_skill was not run
assertion_analysis.notes: Assertion analysis comparing with_skill vs without_skill is blocked until without_skill results are available from a separate PR.

findings: The skill's git diff detection, UID extraction, URL construction, and Playwright screenshot capture all work correctly against a live Grafana instance.

The before-and-after flow works mechanically (file swap → restart → screenshot → restore → restart → screenshot) but the eval's cosmetic change didn't produce a visible difference. Future evals should use a change that affects panel layout.
The no-dashboard-changes early exit logic is sound but was only tested indirectly via git diff path filtering, not end-to-end on a separate branch.
The with_skill vs without_skill comparison cannot be completed in a single agent session. A two-PR approach is recommended.
No iteration-2 is warranted for with_skill results — the skill's procedure produced correct outputs. However, a full benchmark requires without_skill data from a separate PR.
